title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Go Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for postgresql 18 domain schema, keys, constraints, and index intent and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named browser, compiler, network, XML, PostgreSQL, race, load, accessibility, deployment, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for constrained relational feed-service schema. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 6475 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Include tenant ownership in keys, constraints, queries, tests, and audit evidence so cross-tenant references cannot form.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for include tenant ownership in keys, constraints, queries, tests, and audit evidence so cross-tenant references cannot form. For Feed aggregation and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation, keep Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
